sutta: buddha said monks there are these 5 wrong times for striving what 5 herein among is old overcome by old age monks this is the first wrong time for striving among is ill overcome by illness monks this is the second wrong time for striving there is a fair mean crops are bad food is hard to get and it is not easy to get oneself going by cleaning that is picking up food and favors monks is the the third wrong time fear is about perils of bandits and the country folk mount their cards and drive away monks this is the fourth wrong time again monks the sangha is divided the sangha is a community of manga is divided then there is reviling between 1 another accusation between 1 another quarreling between 1 another repudiation between 1 another and they of little faith do not find faith there and the faithful become less faithful monks this is a fifth wrong time for striving monks there are these these are the 5 wrong times for striving monks there are these 5 right times for striving what 5 hearing among is young a mere youth blackhead and blessed with the beauty of youth the heyday of youth monks this is the first right time for striving among has health and well-being a good digestion which is neither over cold nor overheated but even and suitable for strivings monks this is the second right time there is no famine and crops are good food is easy to get and it is easy to get oneself going by cleanings and favors monks this is the third right time men dwell in friendly fellowship together as mingled with milk and water not quarreling but look upon 1 another with friendly eye monks this is the fourth right time again monks the sangha dwell in friendly fellowship together finding comfort in 1 teaching when there is harmony in the sangha then there is no reviling 1 with another nor accusations no quarreling nor repudiation between 1 another but there they are of little faith those of little faith find faith and the faith of the faithful become even more monks this is the fifth right time for striving these monks are the 5 right times for striving
commentary: so here the buddha is talking about the right and the wrong and the right times for striving and especially the we have to make we have to take note now the wrong times for striving the first 1 is old age sometimes some people when they are quite old they want to renounce and become among [Music] and then when they come to monghu they find very difficult to adjust because they have a lot of fixed habits and sometimes they are asked to do this and ask not to do this by certain younger monks because in the seniority depends on the number of years a person has renounced has won the ropes so sometimes a more senior monk can be a younger monk so an old manga is told off by a younger man he might feel very annoying as you heard 1 of the earlier sutas that we read about old monk thinking this fellow can be my son or my grandson and he's telling me what to do what not to do so it's quite difficult for someone in all asia and besides the fixed habits then you become much weaker also uh and become much weaker and uh much more difficult especially if you want to follow the buddha's advice and go and live in lonely places secluded places in a cave up the hill alone by and in all ages much more difficult to do then the second factor is illness when you are ill it's the wrong time to strike but sometimes people are not willing to strive until they are told that they have cancer or something then only they decide no i think i better do some practice but sometimes it's too late now and the third is when there's a famine the fourth when there are bandits about them and the fifth is when the sangha the community of monks is divided then it's uh the lay people would have little faith and there's little faith and there's not that support for the monks so they find it difficult to get the support to live the holy life so these are the wrong times and the reverse and the converse is the right times for striving but the trouble is even though the youth are young when a person is young that's the best time for striving you'll find with most people that's the time when you're most reluctant to strive because you don't see the dukkha you don't you you think you still got a long life ahead of you and there's no urgency to strive you think wait until i'm old then only i'll strive and then when you're old you find it's too late to strive and the nexus is
